enforcement_layer: pre-write-guard.sh write-time DENY on shadowing writes missing `_override_reason`
version-control merge-conflict surfacing on foundation upgrades touching shadowed entries
cross_cutting_meta_rules.failure_mode: Silent collisions between adopter customization and foundation canonical produce unpredictable runtime behavior — rules with the same ID compete, archetype lookups fail, overlay edits silently disable foundation rules. Without the tiebreaker, every foundation upgrade is a potentially-breaking change for adopters with overlays on those entries; without write-time DENY, adopters discover collisions days later in audit reports rather than at the write itself.
cross_cutting_meta_rules.name: Layer-3 Overlay Collision Tiebreaker
cross_cutting_meta_rules.rule_text: When adopter Layer-3 overlay and foundation canonical both declare the same rule ID, archetype enum value, or extensible entry kind: adopter Layer-3 SHADOWS foundation (adopter wins). Foundation declaration is preserved but does not fire. Adopter MUST carry `_override_reason` (free-text, mandatory) on every shadowing entry. Write-time enforcement via pre-write-guard.sh: shadowing writes without `_override_reason` are DENIED at write-time. Per-write `--force-override` flag bypasses the DENY for a single write; no persistent disable. Adopter renames are special-case shadows under a new name (no separate `_rename_history` field; adopter manifest is canonical naming). Foundation upgrades touching shadowed entries surface as merge conflicts in the adopter's vault repo plus `foundation-upgrade-touches-shadowed-entry` warning from librarian governance-parity-audit (audit-time, because foundation upgrades are not adopter writes). ADR-0006 documents the design rationale + 6 rejected alternatives.

Frontmatter is the API every file exposes to the system; this pillar declares the fields, per-type contracts, AND the rules that enforce them. SP13 T-4 dissolved schemas/vault-schema.json into this pillar (types + tier assignment + path routing + archetype binding) plus governance/tagging-rules.json (tag-prefix taxonomy). enforcement_layer: governance/frontmatter-rules.json `types` (type allowlist + per-type required/optional field declarations)
rules.failure_mode: Files written with an unknown `type:` value or with missing Strict-tier required fields drift the schema contract silently. Downstream consumers (auto-routing, librarian, scaffold) cannot reason about the file because the contract has been violated. Without write-time DENY, the schema becomes documentation rather than enforcement — production-scale untagged-file backlogs (~500 files observed in a reference deployment) accumulate before audit catches up. The Tier 2 DENY closes the gap at the moment of capture, returning a structured error that enumerates the missing fields and points to the schema entry.
rules.retired_types_handling: Files declaring a retired `type:` value (currently `engagement`, `project` — retired per ADR-0003) are DENIED with the retirement reference and `replacement` guidance pointing the author to the folder-lineage convention (field-level propagation via `engagement:` + `project:` frontmatter fields plus matching tags). See `retired_types` block.
rules.rule_text: Every Strict-tier vault write MUST declare a `type:` value drawn from the governance/frontmatter-rules.json `types` allowlist (21 canonical type entries; 2 retired types preserved in `retired_types`). For Strict-tier files, the universal required fields (`type`, `tags`, `updated`) AND the per-type `required` field list MUST all be present in the YAML frontmatter. Pre-write-guard DENIES writes failing either check; the DENY message enumerates the missing fields plus the pillar entry path. Adding a new type or extending a required-field list requires R-37 atomic lockstep updating this pillar + the narrative spoke + pre-write-guard SCHEMA_KEY case + post-write-verify type_map in one commit. Standard-tier writes emit a warning instead of DENY (operator triages via librarian frontmatter-coverage-audit); Minimal-tier writes proceed without validation.
rules.source: ENFORCEMENT-MAP.md R-32 + ../research/vault-construction/frontmatter-design.md §The 3 compliance tiers in detail + ../docs/decisions/0001-tiered-compliance.md

enforcement_layer: pre-write-guard.sh folder-placement advisory branch (Tier 1; never blocks)
rules.failure_mode: Files written outside the canonical folder for their declared `type:` produce structural drift that compounds — the file is invisible to type-scoped queries, lives in a folder that does not match its archetype, and creates the impression that 'this type lives anywhere.' Once the precedent lands, follow-on writes drift further. The Tier 1 advisory fires at write-time rather than waiting for weekly audit so the operator sees the misplacement at the moment of capture and can move the file before publishing wikilinks.
rules.rule_text: Files declaring a `type:` value SHOULD reside under the canonical folder for that type per governance/frontmatter-rules.json `path_routing` (the per-archetype lineage rules). Pre-write-guard emits a Tier 1 advisory `[R-33 FOLDER PLACEMENT]` when the file's path does not match the type's expected folder pattern. The advisory never blocks — engagement-specific exceptions, cross-engagement reference docs, and adopter-customized folder structures are legitimate causes for divergence. The advisory text enumerates the expected folder pattern and the current path so the author can decide. Persistent advisory firings on the same type/folder pair surface as `placement-drift` findings in the librarian audit.

coupled_surfaces: governance/foundation-master.json (forward; built from this pillar via tools/build-foundation-master.sh)
enforcement_layer: pre-write-guard.sh atomic-lockstep branch (DENY on partial-surface writes)
rules.failure_mode: Schema-touching changes that land in one surface without the others produce silent drift — the pillar declares a new type the hook does not recognize; the narrative spoke documents fields the pillar has not added; the rule registry references a field name the pillar spells differently. Within weeks the surfaces diverge enough that no single artifact is authoritative. Drift is detectable but already-broken when the audit surfaces it. R-37 closes the gap at write-time by treating partial-surface commits as the structural error they are.
rules.rule_text: The frontmatter pillar binds three coupled surfaces under R-37 atomic lockstep: (1) governance/frontmatter-rules.json (canonical pillar declaring types, tiers, archetype enum, archetype-conditional fields, path-routing rules, and rule registry; SP13 T-4 absorbed the former schemas/vault-schema.json), (2) onboarding/scaffold/vault-architecture/System Governance - Frontmatter.md (narrative spoke), (3) hooks/pre-write-guard.sh SCHEMA_KEY case-statement + hooks/post-write-verify.sh type_map (runtime mirror). Adding, modifying, or retiring a `type:` value, changing a required-field list, introducing a new field class, or updating the tier-compliance contract requires touching ALL THREE surfaces in a single commit. Pre-write-guard's atomic-lockstep branch DENIES writes that touch one surface without the others, with the missing surfaces enumerated. This entry is the pillar-scoped instantiation of the canonical R-37 meta-rule at governance/_index.json `cross_cutting_meta_rules[]`. The interim coupled-surfaces declaration in governance/_index.json carries hooks/config/gate-config.json (until T-6 retirement) and governance/foundation-master.json (forward) per SP13 retirement-order discipline.
rules.source: ENFORCEMENT-MAP.md R-37 (cross-cutting meta-rule; canonical entry in governance/_index.json `cross_cutting_meta_rules[]`) + ../research/vault-construction/frontmatter-design.md §R-37 atomic-lockstep protocol + ../research/vault-construction/enforcement-map-design.md §R-37 atomic-lockstep protocol + ../docs/decisions/0005-two-surface-governance-dual-pattern.md

enforcement_layer: post-write-verify.sh provides-presence advisory (Tier 1; combined with R-38 blockquote-summary block)
exemptions: Archive/** — archived content; provides-declaration is structurally unnecessary
Daily/** — daily notes are temporally-scoped, not capability-scoped
Logs/** — machine-emitted log surfaces; lifecycle is closed at write-time
Inbox/** — aggregation surfaces; provides lives at the canonical destination, not the staging copy
rules.failure_mode: Long-form files (>200 lines) without a `provides:` declaration become opaque to consumers that route by capability rather than by path. The router cannot determine what concepts the file is canonical for; the librarian cannot detect provides-canonicality drift (R-40) because there is no claim to compare against; downstream agents loading the file have no fast way to decide whether the content is relevant. The advisory surfaces the omission at write-time so the author either declares `provides:` deliberately or accepts the file as content-only.
rules.rule_text: Files exceeding 200 lines SHOULD declare a `provides:` array in their YAML frontmatter enumerating the concepts, rules, or capabilities the file is the canonical source for. Post-write-verify emits a Tier 1 advisory `[R-39 PROVIDES PRESENCE]` when a file over the line threshold lacks `provides:` or has `provides: []`. The advisory never blocks — content-only long-form files (raw transcripts, archive imports, daily-note dumps) are legitimate and the author can ignore the advisory. The librarian frontmatter-coverage-audit tracks recurring R-39 firings as `provides-coverage-drift` findings so the operator can triage at session-close.

_exemption_classes._description: SP13 Phase 1c 2026-05-14: foundation declares the EXEMPTION CLASS shape per canonical §G+§H; concrete adopter folder names (engagement-scoped subfolders, project-scoped subfolders, archetype-cluster subfolders, etc.) live in overlay-master.frontmatter.canonical_scope_exemptions[]. Foundation governance stays archetype-agnostic.

classes.name: instance-scoped
classes.rationale: files inside an archetype-cluster instance (engagement / topic / repo / etc.) are scoped to that instance, not vault-wide canonical

classes.name: sub-cluster-scoped
classes.rationale: files inside an archetype's sub-cluster (project / study / epic / etc.) carry sub-cluster-scoped `provides:` vocabulary, not vault-wide canonical

enforcement_layer: drift-allowlist override at hooks/config/drift-allowlist.json (`provides_overlap[]` for deliberate co-canonical pairs)
rules.failure_mode: Two or more canonical-scope files claiming the same `provides:` capability fragment authority. A consumer routing to the capability does not know which file to read; updates to one canonical do not propagate to the other; the two files drift into mutually-contradictory statements within months. Without an audit, the drift is invisible — `grep provides:` returns both files and the consumer picks one by accident. The librarian-audit finding surfaces the collision and asks the operator to either consolidate, archive one source, or register the overlap as deliberate in the drift-allowlist.
rules.rule_text: Within the canonical-scope of the vault (vault root depth-1 + System Governance/** + Skills/**), no two files SHOULD declare the same capability in their `provides:` array. The librarian frontmatter-coverage-audit walks every Standard- and Strict-tier file's `provides:` declaration, builds an inverted index, and emits a `provides-canonicality-drift` finding whenever a capability is claimed by 2+ canonical-scope owners. Severity is `blocking` when any owner sits at vault root or inside System Governance/**; `warning` otherwise. Engagement reference docs, meeting notes, project context files, and daily notes are EXCLUDED from canonical-scope — `provides:` is project-scoped vocabulary there, not vault-wide canonical. Deliberate co-canonical pairs (e.g., a research packet plus its consumer-rendered spoke) register at hooks/config/drift-allowlist.json `provides_overlap[].capability` with rationale + audit-trail entry; allowlist entries mirror into librarian-manifest.json on every run and suppress the finding.
scope_exemptions: Meetings/** — meeting `provides:` is cross-reference helper, not canonical claim (foundation-shipped folder per canonical §F)
Daily/** — daily notes are temporally-scoped (foundation-shipped folder per canonical §F)

enforcement_layer: librarian archetype-consistency audit (audit-time; per-archetype field-coverage verification)
post-write-verify.sh archetype-field advisory (Tier 1; warning on incomplete archetype field-set)
exemptions: Files declaring `tier: minimal` — opt-out from validation
Archive/** — closed lifecycle; archetype-completeness is preserved as a historical record, not a live audit target
Files without any archetype declaration — pre-archetype-era content remains in Standard tier without binding
rules.failure_mode: Files declaring an archetype (via `archetype:` frontmatter field or via per-type entry with archetype binding) but missing required archetype-conditional fields fragment the multi-archetype union model. A consultant archetype file missing `engagement:`/`project:` cannot be filtered to engagement-scoped views. A researcher archetype file missing `topic:`/`study:` cannot resolve to the topic taxonomy. The renamed-dimension overlay (developer's `#repo/*` mapping to the consultant's `#engagement/*` structural slot) breaks at consumption time. The audit closes the gap by walking every archetype-declaring file and verifying the required field set per archetype is fully populated.
rules.rule_text: Files declaring an archetype affiliation (either via the `archetype:` frontmatter field or via a `type:` entry with archetype binding declared in governance/frontmatter-rules.json) MUST carry the full required-field set for that archetype per governance/frontmatter-rules.json `archetype_conditional_fields[<archetype>]` declaration. The librarian archetype-consistency audit walks every archetype-declaring file, computes the per-archetype required-field intersection, and emits `archetype-field-compliance-drift` findings when any required field is missing. Severity is `warning` (Tier 1 advisory) — the file lands; the audit surfaces drift at session-close. Adopters extending the archetype enum via Layer 3 overlay-master.json must extend `archetype_conditional_fields` in the same overlay; otherwise the audit reports the unknown archetype as `archetype-not-in-enum` (R-51 sibling at tagging-rules.json carries the write-time DENY for archetype enum violations). The pair (R-41 advisory at audit-time + R-51 DENY at write-time) is the field-side and tag-side enforcement of the multi-archetype binding contract.

rules.failure_mode: Folders without an _index.md appear as leaves in graph view; LLMs reading the folder have no orientation document and must read each child file individually at full token cost; human readers fall back to filename-by-filename scanning. Pre-mandate baseline in reference deployment showed folder-level navigation breaking past ~10 children and collapsing past ~50. The mandate is structural: post-write-verify auto-bootstrap creates _index.md at first-write to a non-exempt folder — the adopter never has to remember to author the index. Without the auto-bootstrap + Tier 2 sweep, the mandate degrades to advisory and recurring drift accumulates silently between audit runs.
rules.rule_text: Every non-exempt user-facing folder in the vault MUST carry a sibling _index.md at folder root. The _index.md file MUST conform to the frontmatter contract at governance/frontmatter-rules.json #types.index (`type: index`; `tags:` mandatory; `parent_folder:` required at path_depth >= 2 / omitted at depth 1; `updated:` mandatory; SP13 T-4 canonical location absorbed from dissolved schemas/vault-schema.json) AND the body-structure contract at governance/file-type-contracts/_index.md.json (H1 matching folder name; folder-context paragraph 2-4 sentences; contents-enum table with `File | Lines | Type | Description` columns wrapped in sentinel markers). Exempt paths are enumerated in `mandates._index_md.exemption_paths` (positive-list). The mandate is enforced structurally via the three-tier maintenance architecture: Tier 1 post-write-verify auto-bootstrap + live-sync on every Edit/Write tool call (with one-line self-exempt loop guard at hook entry); Tier 2 librarian index-maintain audit sweep on /librarian full + scheduled daily; Tier 3 librarian index-maintain --deep opt-in semantic validation. The two-resource architecture (matcher in this pillar's `mandates._index_md` block + parameters in governance/file-type-contracts/_index.md.json) follows the k8s ValidatingAdmissionPolicy pattern; hooks load the matcher to decide whether to fire, then load the parameter contract to validate body structure.

rules.failure_mode: Silent gitignore bypass — bare-name patterns match only at repo root; deeper instances slip through. A production incident saw ~100 files leak through bare-name gitignore in an initial commit of the reference deployment.
rules.rule_text: Gitignore patterns matching directory names at any depth must use the `**/` prefix. Bare-name patterns match only at repo root; `**/<name>/` matches anywhere in the tree. Documentary-only; promote to pre-commit hook if drift recurs.

rules.failure_mode: Plans without numeric prefix or status break librarian plan-index regeneration. Shame slugs (auto-generated adjective-verb-noun) degrade plan-index, backlog routing, and search-by-name. A production audit found ~60% of plans in Unknown status before R-27 landed.
rules.rule_text: New or edited plan-root files must carry (a) an `NN-` numeric prefix on their top-level path segment AND (b) a status marker (`**Status:**` header bullet, YAML `status:` frontmatter, or `manifest.json` top-level `status` field). Sub-task files, handoffs, and orchestrator artifacts inherit from parent per R-28 and are NOT required to carry status. Tier 2 DENY at write-time.

exemptions: Plan-root files at depth 2 (spec.md, tasks.md, handoff.md, 00-ideation-brief.md, README.md, manifest.json) — they ARE the parent
handoff.md at any depth (append-only session record)
Files under tests/ (synthetic fixtures, not plan state)
Files under _orchestrator/ (orchestrator runtime, not plan state)
rules.failure_mode: Sub-plan files at depth ≥ 3 without `parent_plan:` frontmatter are invisible to plan-tree walkers — librarian cannot resolve ancestry. Librarian-audit only; drift surfaced as findings, not write-time block. Pre-formalization, ~60 sub-plan files used the convention informally without resolver.
rules.field_value_format: top-level plan slug (no path, no extension)
rules.rule_text: Sub-task files at depth ≥ 3 under `{plans_root}/` must carry `parent_plan: <top-level-slug>` frontmatter. Value is the top-level plan slug (no path, no extension). Plan-root files at depth 2, handoff.md at any depth, files under tests/, and files under _orchestrator/ are EXEMPT. Tier 1 advisory — librarian-audit only; conditional escalation if drift recurs.

enforcement_layer: pre-write-guard.sh tag-validation branch (system-utility dimension DENY on near-match)
rules.failure_mode: Near-match drift in system-utility dimensions fragments operational subtypes within weeks. Two runs of the same routine emit two different tag values (e.g., #log/backlog-hygiene vs #log/backlog-cleanup), and the user-side graph filter splits routine activity across variant spellings. The discipline restores stability: every routine activity uses the STABLE, canonical tag value registered at first emission, and the hook DENIES near-match drift.
rules.rule_text: Tags within system-utility dimensions (#log/*, #status/*) must match a value registered in governance/log-subtype-registry.json. Near-match writes (Levenshtein distance ≤ 2 or substring containment of an existing canonical value) are DENIED with a 'did you mean #<dim>/<canonical>?' suggestion. Genuinely new subtypes register explicitly via the prompt-and-commit pattern at first emission. The discipline mirrors the canonical-tag-per-concept principle on the system-utility surface where LLM stochasticity would otherwise fragment the vocabulary.

enforcement_layer: Runtime allowlist authority = union of foundation-master.tagging.taxonomy.dimension_prefixes (foundation; SP13 T-4 absorbed from dissolved vault-schema._tag_prefixes; SP13 Phase 1c 2026-05-14 abstracted user-facing to []) + overlay-master.tagging.taxonomy.dimension_prefixes (adopter; populated by onboarding archetype seeds at install time). Hook union-resolve lands in SP13 T-7 + T-8 scope.
rules.failure_mode: Freeform tagging produces folksonomy drift within months at scale. Synonymous content scatters across variant spellings (meeting / meetings / meeting-notes), case variants (client-a / ClientA / client_a), and one-off ad-hoc dimensions (#priority/*, #urgency/*). The user-side graph degrades silently — every file carries tags, no query returns the right set. Write-time DENY converts the silent failure into a visible governance signal at the moment of capture.
rules.rule_text: Every tag MUST match the hierarchical grammar `#dimension/value` where dimension is one of the registered prefixes in the runtime allowlist (union of foundation-master.tagging.taxonomy.dimension_prefixes + overlay-master.tagging.taxonomy.dimension_prefixes) and value is a kebab-case slug matching [a-z0-9-]. The pre-write-guard hook DENIES any tag that fails the regex or starts with an unregistered prefix. The DENY message enumerates the non-conforming tags. Adopters either pick a tag from the registered vocabulary, omit the tag (the file remains legible to Claude via its frontmatter fields), or extend overlay-master.tagging.taxonomy.dimension_prefixes via `/govern register --kind tag-extension` (post-SP13 T-5).
rules.source: ENFORCEMENT-MAP.md R-32 + ../research/vault-construction/tagging-strategy.md Rules 2 and 4 (hierarchical prefix grammar + no freeform tags)

rules.exempt_path_semantics: positive-list — unenumerated top-level paths default to the advisory firing (NOT auto-exempt). Adding a new exempt path requires R-37 atomic lockstep updating this rule + pre-write-guard.sh + the narrative spoke + foundation-master.json bundle composition.
rules.failure_mode: Non-exempt files written without tags become orphans in Obsidian graph view — invisible to user-side queries even though the file is legible to Claude-side surfaces via frontmatter fields. Pre-write tag-presence advisory surfaces the omission at the moment of write rather than at the weekly audit, closing a ~7-day detection lag.
rules.r47_exempt_paths_provenance: SP13 T-4 canonical union (closes 7-vs-13 drift): 7 entries formerly canonical here + 5 infrastructure entries formerly only in hooks/config/gate-config.json `r47.exempt_paths`. SP13 Phase 1c 2026-05-14: 4 non-adopter-vault entries removed (Tags/** Obsidian artifact retired per canonical §G; _test* loose foundation-repo-only pattern; _orchestrator/** plan-tree; tests/** foundation-repo only) leaving 8 adopter-vault-relevant entries. The composed runtime view (`foundation-master.json :: r47_exempt_paths_composed`) builds the union deterministically from pillar declarations; gate-config.json continues to be the canonical runtime source until SP13 T-6 retirement consummates the migration.
rules.rule_text: Every non-exempt vault write MUST carry a non-empty `tags:` array in its frontmatter. Pre-write-guard emits a Tier 1 advisory ([R-47 TAG PRESENCE]) when the field is missing or empty (tags: []). Distinguishes 'missing' (no tags: line) vs 'empty' (line present, no items). Exempt paths are enumerated in the registry's `r47_exempt_paths` array — they cover archive surfaces, ideation-brief symlinks, machine-emitted scratch zones, and system infrastructure paths whose tag-absence is structurally legitimate. (SP13 T-4 2026-05-14: r47_exempt_paths absorbs the 5 additional infrastructure paths formerly in gate-config.json `r47.exempt_paths` to form the canonical union. SP13 Phase 1c 2026-05-14: dropped 4 non-adopter-vault entries — Tags/** (Obsidian Make.md artifact; .gitignored per SP03 D3), _test*, _orchestrator/** (plan-tree, not adopter vault), tests/** (foundation-repo only) — leaving 8 entries covering adopter-vault foundation surfaces + system infrastructure paths. foundation-master.json composes the runtime r47_exempt_paths_composed view; gate-config.json retires under SP13 T-6.) The librarian tag-coverage-audit walks the vault at audit time and surfaces longer-running orphans + near-miss patterns (#scope/decisions vs #scope/decision — one is likely a typo) as advisory findings the operator triages.

enforcement_layer: librarian tag-coverage-audit (audit-time consolidation prompt at 80% threshold)
rules.failure_mode: Cap-widening defeats the working-memory rationale silently. An adopter activates a new engagement, user-facing tag count approaches 25, and the path of least resistance is 'raise the cap to 30.' Past 25, decision fatigue produces variant-creation rather than canonical-term selection, the vocabulary fragments, and user-side recall collapses. The cap is structural, not aspirational — silent widening is rejected; deliberate Layer 3 override is supported with audit trail.
rules.override_mechanism: Layer 3 overlay-master.json :: tagging_cap_override (post-SP13 T-5 slot) with rationale + audit-trail entry
rules.rule_text: Total count of distinct values across user-facing dimensions (declared at runtime by union of foundation-master.tagging.taxonomy.user_facing_dimensions + overlay-master.tagging.taxonomy.user_facing_dimensions) MUST stay under 25. System-utility dimensions (log, status) are exempt — they are machine-emitted and governed by the log-subtype registry (R-05). The librarian tag-coverage-audit emits an advisory when user-facing usage approaches 80% of the cap (≥20 active values), surfaces a consolidation prompt with retire-candidate recommendations, and requires deliberate Layer 3 override (`overlay-master.json :: tagging_cap_override` with rationale + audit-trail entry) before silent widening can proceed. (SP13 Phase 1c 2026-05-14: foundation user_facing_dimensions abstracted to [] per canonical §G+§H; adopter populates via onboarding archetype seeds at install time.)

enforcement_layer: governance/frontmatter-rules.json#archetype_enum (registered archetypes authority — SP13 T-4 absorbed from dissolved vault-schema._archetype_enum)
Layer 3 overlay-master.json archetype_extensions (adopter-declared custom archetype declaration)
rules.failure_mode: Files declaring an archetype field value outside the registered archetype enum drift the multi-archetype union model. Cross-archetype queries fail because the archetype field carries an unrecognized value; the renamed-dimension overlay (e.g., #repo/* in developer archetype) cannot resolve back to its structural slot; the per-archetype synonym dictionary breaks at consumption time.
rules.rule_text: Any frontmatter `archetype:` field value MUST match a value registered in governance/frontmatter-rules.json#archetype_enum (consultant | researcher | developer | manager) OR an adopter-declared custom archetype in Layer 3 overlay-master.json archetype_extensions. Pre-write-guard DENIES writes with an unrecognized archetype value. The DENY message enumerates the registered values and points to the Layer 3 declaration path. The rule binds the multi-archetype union architectural commitment to write-time — adding a new archetype requires R-37 atomic lockstep updating governance/frontmatter-rules.json#archetype_enum + this rule + the narrative spoke + the archetype-conditional field extensions.
rules.source: ../research/vault-construction/vault-construction-principles.md commitment 4 (multi-archetype union) + ../research/vault-construction/tagging-strategy.md §Per-archetype dimension relabeling + spec §Archetype-binding rule contract

taxonomy._description: Faceted tag taxonomy (SP13 T-4 2026-05-14: canonical authority; absorbed from dissolved vault-schema._tag_prefixes + _tag_prefixes_meta. SP13 Phase 1c 2026-05-14: foundation user_facing_dimensions abstracted to [] empty per canonical §G+§H — concrete user-facing dimensions belong to adopter overlay-master.tagging.taxonomy.dimension_prefixes + .user_facing_dimensions, populated by onboarding archetype seeds at install time). Foundation ships taxonomy SHAPE + system-utility dimensions (status, log) governed by log-subtype-registry per ADR-0004. Runtime allowlist = foundation + overlay-master union (hook union-resolve implementation lands in SP13 T-7 hook audit + T-8 install scope).

archetype-template._description: Scaffold templates at `onboarding/templates/<archetype>/` consumed by install. Pre-populated frontmatter; R-32 100% conformant. Anti-drift principle: archetype templates ship pre-populated upfront; user-added files inherit defaults via templates.

index.body_structure_contract_rationale: Body-structure rules (H1, folder-context paragraph, contents-enum table with column shape + sentinel markers + ordering convention) live in the per-file-type contract following the k8s ValidatingAdmissionPolicy paramKind pattern (matcher in pillar/schema; parameters in separate replaceable resource). See ../research/vault-construction/_index.md-design.md + ../target-state/_index.md-design/structural-requirements.md.

parent_folder.condition_semantics: depth is the number of path segments between the vault root and the file; depth 1 = `<cluster>/_index.md` (parent_folder omitted because the parent IS the vault root); depth >= 2 = `<cluster>/<instance>/_index.md` or deeper (parent_folder mandatory).
parent_folder.rationale: Navigation-side lineage pointer auto-populated by post-write-verify auto-bootstrap; librarian index-maintain audits for path-vs-frontmatter drift. Distinct from the content-side folder-lineage convention (`engagement:`/`project:`) which uses path_routing. See ../research/vault-construction/_index.md-design.md §parent_folder.

packet._description: Research context packets at all four altitudes (system / engagement / topic / initiative). System-altitude packets canonical at `claude-stem/research/vault-construction/`; engagement/topic/initiative at user vault. Surfaces 180-day `last_reviewed` cadence (system) / 90-day (topic) / continuous (engagement) / closed-at-plan-close (initiative).
